tldr: Switch from Jumio's enterprise KYC/AML suite to Persona's modular, rule-based identity platform for more flexible flows and clearer per-verification economics.
why_switch: Want modular, configurable verification flows instead of a fixed enterprise pipeline
Prefer pay-per-successful-verification economics over seat-style contracts
Need rule-based decisioning you can iterate on quickly
Looking for stronger self-serve developer experience and SDKs
Consolidating IDV, KYB, and document checks under one configurable provider
what_to_export: Verified user records with status and timestamps
Document scans and metadata where retention policy allows
Decisioning rules and thresholds currently configured in Jumio
Operator audit logs and case notes
Mapping between Jumio reference IDs and your internal user IDs
export_method: Use Jumio's official export mechanisms (admin console and/or API as documented) to extract verification records and audit data. Treat all PII as production-sensitive and coordinate with your DPO on retention. Pull configuration (rules, thresholds, branding) into a written spec rather than trying to migrate it directly — Persona's model is different.
import_method: Provision a Persona tenant following their official documentation, model your verification journey using Persona's configurable flows, and load reference data via their documented APIs. Persona supports API export per their documented data-portability story, so plan a parallel-run period: route a percentage of new traffic to Persona, compare outcomes against Jumio, then ramp up. Wire webhooks for verification events into your existing case management.

concept_mapping.note: Re-design the journey rather than porting it 1:1
Model expected volume to compare TCO honestly
Confirm exact coverage in Persona's docs for your jurisdictions
Replace mobile/web SDK integration; UX QA required

gotchas: Pricing models are different (seat vs per-verification); model both at expected volume
Verification UX will visibly change; run A/B or shadow flows before full cutover
Rules don't port directly — rebuild them and re-validate against historical decisions
AML/ongoing-monitoring coverage details vary; confirm jurisdictional fit in official docs
Keep Jumio archives for the full statutory retention window
Plan operator retraining for the new case management surface

email.subject: Jumio -> Persona migration proposal
email.body:
Hi team,

I'd like to migrate our identity verification stack from Jumio to Persona. The driver is configurable, modular flows and clearer per-verification economics.

Plan:
1. Export verification records, decisions, and audit logs from Jumio per their docs.
2. Document current rules and thresholds as a spec (not a literal port).
3. Stand up a Persona tenant per their official docs and rebuild flows + rules using their primitives.
4. Run Persona in parallel for a traffic slice; compare outcomes vs Jumio.
5. Cut over by segment, then decommission Jumio after the retention window.

Estimated effort 4-10 weeks. Main risks: UX change, rule rebuild correctness, and jurisdictional coverage parity.

Thanks
